response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile senior engineer with strong testing culture and systems-level performance experience, but his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his current skills list. His career since has centered on web and full-stack work in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, UML-driven architecture work, or IEC 62443 / SDL secure-development practices. For a role whose core is hands-on C++/STL development for industrial automation platforms, the mismatch on the primary competency and the domain is too large despite his general seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus proven mentoring through workshops, courses, and code katas
Long, continuous senior-level track record across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including event-driven platform architecture
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014-2015); C++ is absent from his listed skills
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, OPC UA, or IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle exposure
Recent career trajectory is full-stack web (TypeScript/Node.js), diverging from high-performance C++ platform engineering

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 security background
Strong systems performance engineering and mentoring record
